behavior_summary: This script implements a single 'U8P_GEMOFPRO::look' process that displays a short descriptive text for the object: the player-facing bark 'blue field passage gem '.
There are no conditionals, menus, external calls, or flag reads/writes; the process shows the description and then suspends/terminates.
The routine is purely presentational: it exists to provide the examine/U8P_GEMOFPRO::look text for the game object and does not modify game state.
notable_control_flow: Single Bark node emitting the description string followed by a suspend/terminate node (standard end-of-process behavior).
No branching, loops, or calls to other internal/external functions are present.
open_questions: Whether U8P_GEMOFPRO::look is also invoked by an area-trigger (enterFastArea) in some map contexts — common for U8P_GEMOFPRO::look-type processes but not explicit in the script (inference: likely but unconfirmed).
Whether the trailing space in the bark text ('blue field passage gem ') is intentional (stylistic) or an artifact of disassembly.
If the object has alternate inspect states (e.g., different descriptions when flags change) — none are present here, but higher-level game code could override behavior.

main_function.name: U8P_GEMOFPRO::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/U8P_GEMOFPRO::look description for the object by emitting a single player-facing bark string.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player performs a 'U8P_GEMOFPRO::look' or examine action on the object (canonical for processType 0x0208).
May also be invoked when an actor enters the object's fast-area trigger region in engines that reuse U8P_GEMOFPRO::look handlers for area-entry (inferred).

function_map.name: U8P_GEMOFPRO::look
function_map.role: object examine/description handler that emits the one-line bark text
